fix: revert limiter to RMS-only, remove broken peak-sum blend

Peak-sum normalization was wildly too aggressive for additive synthesis —
sum-of-absolute-amplitudes with 1024 oscillators is orders of magnitude
larger than actual peak output, crushing the signal at every setting.

Sprint 7-8: SDKs, webhooks, dashboard, feature flags, and code review fixes

Sprint 7 (Growth):
- T7.1: Python/JavaScript/Go client SDKs with typed models and auth helpers
- T7.2: Webhook system with HMAC signatures, retry backoff, delivery log
- T7.3: Admin dashboard with usage analytics, customer management, health metrics
- T7.4: Feature flags with org/user/percentage targeting and admin API

Sprint 8 (Code Review Fixes):
- T8.1: Fix webhook_deliveries logic bug, add webhook_exists(), asyncio.gather safety
- T8.2: Decouple dashboard from private stores via public APIs in billing/user_routes
- T8.3: Type safety — RuleType enum, Role enum validation at API boundary
- T8.4: Feature flag duplicate guard, ValueError handling, shared admin_headers fixture

Simplify pass:
- Replace private _users imports in admin_routes.py and roles.py with public APIs
- Fix incorrect rate limit docs in api_docs.py (was 30/min for all tiers)
- Extract _to_response() helper in webhook_routes.py
- Add update_user() to user_routes.py; dashboard.update_customer uses Role enum
- Add node_modules/ to .gitignore

661 tests passing, 0 architecture violations.

commit.message:
Fix Intercom chat: identify user on boot instead of anonymous update

Intercom was booted anonymously in index.html, then update() was called
with user identity — but Intercom ignores identity on anonymous sessions.
Now we shutdown the anonymous session and reboot with the persona's
name, email, and user_id so the widget recognizes the logged-in user.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

T4: numeric validation engine

Crea backend/rocco/numericRecognizer.js:
- extractElectricalValues(text): estrae valori tecnici strutturati
  voltage (V, kV), current (A), differential (mA), resistance (Ω, kΩ, MΩ),
  power (W, kW), ip (IP44, IP65...), temperature (°C), pressure (bar)
- formatElectricalValues(): stringa leggibile per contesto LLM
- checkAnomalies(): warnings per tensioni MT/AT (kV), soglie diff. anomale

Integra in server.js:
- Aggiunge valori tecnici estratti all'engineText (contesto LLM)
- Attiva safety lock se tensione MT/AT rilevata (messaggio CEI 11-27)
- Try/catch isolato: errore non blocca la risposta

commit.message:
feat(ads): overhaul Paid Advertising with LinkedIn, real estimates, and 5 new AI tools

Backend:
- Convert POST /generate to SSE streaming (goodbye 30s blocking spinner)
- Fix POST /score-ad: async/await, remove fake hardcoded fallback, real errors
- Fix POST /generate-headline-variations: async/await, remove fake fallback, real errors
- Add POST /adapt-platform SSE: adapt any campaign to a different platform
- Add POST /negative-keywords SSE: generate 25-30 Google negative keywords with match types
- Add POST /video-script SSE: 15/30/60s video ad scripts for TikTok/YouTube with hook, body, CTA
- Add parseAdJSON() helper: strips code fences, falls back to regex match

Prompts:
- Add LinkedIn Ads platform instructions (intro text, headlines, lead gen form, audience targeting by job title/industry/seniority)
- Improve all platform prompts with character limits and platform-specific guidance

Frontend:
- Add LinkedIn Ads as 4th platform (Sponsored Content, Lead Gen Form, Conversation Ad, Thought Leader)
- Fix reach estimates: platform-specific benchmarks (Google $1.20-$4.00 CPC, Meta $0.50-$1.80, TikTok $0.25-$0.80, LinkedIn $6.00-$14.00)
- Add Saved Campaigns (History) tab: loads from API, load/delete, accessible from both screens
- Add Ad Preview: CSS mockup of Google search result, Meta feed card, TikTok overlay, LinkedIn sponsored post
- Add Multi-Platform Adapt: one-click campaign adaptation to other platforms, shows adapted copy inline
- Add UTM Link Builder: auto-generates utm_source/medium/campaign/content parameters
- Add Negative Keywords panel (Google only): grouped by category with match types, copy-all
- Add Video Script Generator (TikTok/YouTube): structured hook → body → CTA timeline view
- Add per-item Copy buttons on all headlines/descriptions/primary texts
- Replace empty catch{} blocks with toast error notifications
- Platform capabilities section now shows 9 capabilities including new ones

commit.message:
fix: prevent automatic plan execution — launchSwarm forced to false

Plans must ONLY execute when the user clicks "Execute Plan" or explicitly
tells the AI to start. The SDK parameter launchSwarm is now forcefully
set to false in the ExitPlanMode permission handler, preventing any
automatic plan execution regardless of what the AI requests.

Also includes cleanup of debug logging in message loop.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

commit.message:
fix: block configure mode on secondary cluster node

On a real SRX, the secondary node rejects config changes — RG0 is the
config authority. Block 'configure' in both the local CLI and gRPC
EnterConfigure RPC when the node is not primary for RG0.

commit.message:
Initial commit: Venture Signal Tracker

Chrome extension + Express backend for startup intelligence gathering.
10 parallel TinyFish web agents per company, real-time SSE streaming,
Supabase DB, Resend email reports with CSV, daily cron scheduler.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: auto-deposit ETH to burner during delegation + expose getBurnerBalance

DelegationButton now sends a 0.0005 ETH deposit from MetaMask to the
burner wallet after the delegation TX succeeds (second MetaMask popup).
This ensures the burner has gas funds before navigating to character
creation, eliminating the "Insufficient funds" error on first TX.

If the deposit fails (user rejects or has no ETH), delegation still
completes and a warning is shown — user can fund via settings later.

Also exposes getBurnerBalance() from MUDContext so DelegationButton can
force an immediate balance refresh after deposit, preventing App.tsx
from flashing the WalletDetailsModal for a stale zero balance.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add backend API endpoints, routing screen, building map, and app state machine

- Implement all 4 FastAPI endpoints: /api/voice/connect (LiveKit/stub),
  /api/checkin (webhook), /api/checkin/status (polling), /api/checkin/reset

Feature/mermaid separate file (#37)

* feat: add SOQL/SOSL query template builder with Mermaid ERD (v2.8.0)

Adds SOQLTemplateService that generates a companion Markdown file
alongside each Treecipe recipe YAML. Each file is scoped to its
relationship tree and includes:
- Mermaid erDiagram (entities, Lookup and MasterDetail cardinality)
- Base SELECT queries for all object fields
- Child-to-parent queries with relationship dot-notation traversal
- Parent-to-child subqueries (intra-tree only)
- Record type filtered query variants
- SOSL templates scoped to text-type fields

Cross-tree isolation enforced: filtered wrapper passed per tree so
ERD and subqueries never bleed objects from other hierarchies.
38 new unit tests added.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: complete MLOps pipeline for dental OPG cavity detection

Full end-to-end production pipeline:
- Stage 1: Data ingestion from Google Drive (gdown + fallback)
- Stage 2: Auto-detection of YOLO/COCO/VOC annotation formats + validation
- Stage 3: Multi-format data transformation with medical-safe augmentation
  (CLAHE, brightness, slight rotation - NO flips for X-ray orientation)
- Stage 4: YOLOv8 training with MLflow experiment tracking
- Stage 5: Model evaluation (mAP50, mAP50-95, P, R, F1, visual reports)

MLOps infrastructure:
- DVC pipeline with GDrive remote for data/model versioning
- MLflow experiment tracking with artifact logging
- GitHub Actions CI (lint, test, docker) + CD (HuggingFace deploy)
- Scheduled weekly retraining workflow
- Pre-commit hooks (black, isort, flake8)

Deployment:
- Gradio app for HuggingFace Spaces
- HuggingFace Hub model upload
- Docker multi-stage build

Testing:
- 18 unit tests (100% passing)
- Integration tests for pipeline components

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

v0.6.0 - Lodash 4.x upgrade, deprecation notice, and other archaeological discoveries

Updated lodash from 3.x to ^4.17.23 and fixed the mass extinction of
method aliases that came with it (include->includes, contains->includes,
any->some). Updated all lodash/index.js refs to lodash/lodash.js because
lodash 4 dropped UMD from its main entry point like it was a mix tape
from an old ex.

Bumped version to 0.6.0 across package.json, bower.json, and
component.json — yes, both of those still exist, like finding a
Blockbuster membership card in your junk drawer...ah, memories.

Added deprecation notice to README. This project has been optimistically promising "...More to Come..." since 2014. It did not, in fact, come. Well, technically, it kinda did with postal v3, but who's counting?

Changes:
- package.json, bower.json, component.json: lodash ^4.17.23, version 0.6.0
- src/: _.include -> _.includes, _.contains -> _.includes, _.any -> _.some
- karma.conf.js: lodash/index.js -> lodash/lodash.js (UMD build)
- example/*.html: same lodash path fix
- lib/: rebuilt from source
- README.md: added deprecation notice pointing to postal.js v3

commit.message:
Free subdomain when Stripe checkout session expires

When a user starts signup but never completes payment, the provisioned-sites
record stays in pending_payment status indefinitely, locking the subdomain.
Handle the checkout.session.expired Stripe webhook event to delete these
stale records and release the subdomain for others.

Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: allow same-name skills from different sources with qualified storage names

When two skills from different sources share the same frontmatter name
(e.g., both named "commit"), they now coexist using source-qualified
storage names (e.g., alice-skills--commit, bob-tools--commit). The
existing skill is automatically renamed when a conflict is first
detected. Workspace validation prevents two skills with the same
frontmatter name from being active in the same workspace.

Also fixes the updater to correctly match qualified storage names
against remote frontmatter names, preventing false "skill removed"
detection during updates.

commit.message:
feat(agent): replace stdout parsing with PermissionRequest hook relay

Replaces the fragile regex-based stdout parsing approach with Claude
Code's native PermissionRequest hook. The hook receives structured JSON
(tool_name, tool_input), relays through CV-Hub prompt API, polls for
response, and returns allow/deny decision. Eliminates ANSI parsing,
piped stdio, and pattern matching entirely.

Bump to v0.7.16
